# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
| ------- | --------- |
| 0.1.x   | ✅ Yes    |

Older versions are not supported. Please upgrade to the latest release before reporting a vulnerability.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.**

mcpguard uses [GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing-information-about-vulnerabilities/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability). To report a vulnerability:

1. Go to the **Security** tab of this repository.
2. Click **"Report a vulnerability"**.
3. Fill in the advisory form with as much detail as possible.

We will receive your report privately and can collaborate with you through GitHub's secure advisory workflow before any public disclosure.

### What to include

- Description of the vulnerability and its impact
- Steps to reproduce (a minimal proof-of-concept is ideal)
- Affected versions
- Any suggested mitigations or patches

### What NOT to include in public issues

To protect mcpguard users and the packages they scan, **never post the following publicly**:

- Actual secrets, API keys, or tokens found during a scan
- Full contents of private or unpublished packages
- Details of unpatched vulnerabilities before coordinated disclosure

If you accidentally exposed sensitive information in a public issue, please [contact GitHub Support](https://support.github.com) to have the content removed.

## Response Timeline

| Milestone | Target |
| --------- | ------ |
| Acknowledgment | **48 hours** after receiving your report |
| Initial assessment | **7 days** |
| Patch / advisory | **30 days** for high/critical; **90 days** for lower severity |

We follow [coordinated disclosure](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Vulnerability_Disclosure_Cheat_Sheet.html). We will credit you in the advisory and in the Hall of Fame below unless you prefer to remain anonymous.
